name: propose_goal
description: If someone clearly says they want to work on something (e.g. 'I want to swear less', 'we want to be kinder at dinner'), you may draft a goal. Drafts DO NOT start tracking — they need explicit confirmation. Always tell the person you've drafted it and ask if they'd like to confirm. Use snake_case metric_key (e.g. swear_count_per_session, kindness_moments_count) only if you can clearly count it across sessions.

owner_type.description: 'member' for an individual goal; 'whanau' for a family goal.

owner_id.description: UUID of the member when owner_type is 'member'. Omit for whānau goals.

title.description: Short, plain-language title. e.g. 'Decrease swearing during family time'.

description.description: 1 short sentence of context — why the person wants this.

metric_key.description: Optional snake_case metric to count per session. Omit if there's no clean count.

direction.description: Which way 'good' looks for this metric.

rationale.description: 1 sentence on why this goal arose in conversation, for the audit trail.
